statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input consists of a single line containing an integer n.
statement.outputFormat: If the number is a power of two, print Yes, otherwise print No.
statement.notes: Time complexity O(log n), space complexity O(1).

hints.text: A power of two has exactly one bit set to 1 in its binary representation.
You can use the bitwise AND operator to check for the presence of a particular bit.

editorial.title: Correcting a Faulty Binary Exponentiation
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved using bitwise operations to check if a number is a power of two.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Check all numbers up to n to see if any of them are powers of two.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use bitwise operations to reduce time complexity.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the bitwise AND operator to check for the presence of a particular bit.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Check the binary representation of the number.
commonMistakes: Overlooking the possibility of n being 1
